head.hi: The Earl of Elgin to Governor Sir W. MacGregor.

div.p: Referring to my telegram of even date, please state whether your ministers, in the event of the negotiations for a modus vivendi breaking down, are prepared to indemnify His Majesty’s Government against any claims for compensation which may be preferred by United States Government, and which it may not be possible consistently with a fair interpretation of treaty rights to refuse; also whether, in the event of a reference to arbitration becoming in the opinion of His Majesty’s Government necessary or desirable, your ministers will agree to such reference, and undertake to meet expenses of arbitration and pay the award, if any.
